hypothesis: Using cointegration analysis as a signal filter for mean reversion would improve signal quality by confirming that oversold stocks have statistically validated mean-reverting pair relationships

implementation.approach: Boost-only filter: when a mean-reversion candidate has cointegrated pairs with confirming spread z-scores, boost confidence by 0.08. No penalty for contradiction.

pair_discovery.method: Engle-Granger cointegration test with Spearman pre-filter

bugs_found_and_fixed: Bug 1: abs(correlation) allowed negative correlations to pass - fixed to positive-only >= 0.70
Bug 2: p-value 0.05 too loose - tightened to 0.01
Bug 3: Contradiction penalty fired on near-zero spreads - set penalty to 0.0 (boost-only)
root_cause_of_failure: Low pair coverage: only 77/159 tickers have cointegrated pairs
Sparse signal overlap: only 4 of ~12 typical MR trade tickers have pair coverage
Average 0.5 pairs checked per MR trade - most signals have zero pairs to validate
Marginal boost pushes 2 borderline MR signals over 0.75 confidence threshold
Extra trades crowd out higher-quality TF trades (max 5 position constraint)
Net effect: TF lost $14.23 in PnL from position slot crowding
future_considerations: Revisit if universe grows significantly (more pair candidates)
Consider penalty-only mode (block trades that lack pair confirmation)
Useful if capital grows enough for dedicated pairs positions
decision: Filter disabled in config. Code retained. v8.2 baseline remains optimal.
